title: heterotheca echioides var. bolanderi
intro: Heterotheca echioides var. bolanderi, also known as Bolander's false goldenaster, is a perennial herb that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Bolander's false goldenaster include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and respiratory issues.
